series.title: [PATCH] net/ftgmac100: preserve full VLAN TCI in TX tag insertion

review.summary: The code change itself is plausible, but the submitted patch is malformed and not applyable. The mail body contains corrupted whitespace/encoding in the diff, which matches the reported checkpatch failure ("patch seems to be corrupt") and the failed `git am` status. This needs to be resent in clean `git format-patch`/`git send-email` form before functional review can proceed.

A.details: Patch format is broken: checkpatch reports corruption and `git am` failed.

B.details: Changing the TX VLAN TCI parameter from `uint8_t` to `uint16_t` is consistent with the bug description and is likely the intended fix.

C.details: Build/test validation could not start because the patch did not apply.

D.details: Runtime and regression risk cannot be assessed from an unapplied patch.

E.details: Resend the patch with clean ASCII whitespace and unwrapped diff lines, then rerun review.

findings.title: Patch is corrupted in transit and cannot be applied
findings.description: The diff contains corrupted whitespace/encoding (visible non-ASCII spacing and altered indentation), which is consistent with the provided checkpatch error and the failed `git am` status. As submitted, maintainers cannot apply, build, or test this change, so it must be resent in a clean mail-ready format.
findings.suggestion: Regenerate and resend the patch using `git format-patch` and `git send-email` (or attach the raw `.patch` file without any editor/mail-client reformatting). Ensure the message body and diff use plain ASCII spaces only, with no wrapped lines or UTF-8 non-breaking spaces.
findings.confidence_reason: The submission explicitly reports checkpatch corruption and failed `git am`, and the patch text visibly shows spacing/encoding damage in headers and hunk context.

issues: ERROR: patch seems to be corrupt (line wrapped?)

diff:
From git@z Thu Jan  1 00:00:00 1970
Subject: [PATCH] net/ftgmac100: preserve full VLAN TCI in TX tag insertion
From: linhuang <linhuang@ruijie.com.cn>
Date: Fri, 27 Mar 2026 15:39:35 +0800
Message-Id: <1471e413d3589b6734819bda2b59b725b74f273a.d551129b.99fc.47f6.86dc.0759227268a2@feishu.cn>
MIME-Version: 1.0
Content-Type: text/plain; charset="utf-8"
Content-Transfer-Encoding: 8bit

The VLAN tag insertion helper took vlan_tci as an 8-bit value, while
the descriptor provides a 16-bit VLAN TCI. This truncated egress tags
and caused VLAN ID corruption (for example, 4088/0x0ff8 became
248/0x00f8 at the receiver).

Change vlan_tci to uint16_t in the TX VLAN insertion path so the full
802.1Q TCI is preserved and transmitted correctly.

Signed-off-by: linhuang <linhuang@ruijie.com.cn>
---
 hw/net/ftgmac100.c | 2 +-
 1 file changed, 1 insertion(+), 1 deletion(-)

diff --git a/hw/net/ftgmac100.c b/hw/net/ftgmac100.c
index 2f05bba11d..b3da48c343 100644
--- a/hw/net/ftgmac100.c
+++ b/hw/net/ftgmac100.c
@@ -498,7 +498,7 @@ static int ftgmac100_write_bd(FTGMAC100Desc *bd, dma_addr_t addr)
 }

 static int ftgmac100_insert_vlan(FTGMAC100State *s, int frame_size,
-                                  uint8_t vlan_tci)
+                                 uint16_t vlan_tci)
 {
     uint8_t *vlan_hdr = s->frame + (ETH_ALEN * 2);
     uint8_t *payload = vlan_hdr + sizeof(struct vlan_header);
--
2.34.1
disclaimer: LLM-generated draft. Not an authoritative review.
